title: Praji
content:
{{Top|legends=Nahdonnis Praji}} {{Update|[[The Sith of Datawork]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:CommanderPraji.png]] |name=Praji |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human]]{{Film|IV}} |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{EncyclopediaCite|The Rise of the Rebellion and the Battle of Yavin}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Officer Corps]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lord Vader, the battle station plans are not aboard this ship and no transmissions were made. An escape pod was jettisoned during the fighting but no life forms were aboard.|Commander Praji, to Darth Vader|}} '''Praji''' was a [[Gender|male]] [[human]] who served as a [[commander]] in the [[Imperial Military]].[[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]] In [[1 BBY]], he served on the ''[[Devastator]]'', [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader's]] [[flagship]], where he was tasked with capturing [[R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO]] on [[Tatooine]]. ==Biography== ===Imperial commander=== Praji grew up in wealth and privilege, just like many of his rivals. After distinguishing himself both academically and as an officer, he became an honored [[commander]] in the [[Imperial Officer Corps|Officer Corps]] of the [[Galactic Empire]].{{Topps|set=2017 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Reborn|cardname=#ANH-26 - Commander Praji|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/135181/cid/10118834}} In [[1 BBY]],{{FilmDate|IV|1}} he was stationed on board the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Devastator]]'' as key [[aide]] to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] when the [[Star Destroyer]] pursued the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|rebel]] [[CR90 corvette]] ''[[Tantive IV]]'' following the [[Battle of Scarif]]. During the battle, the [[Death Star plans|plans]] to the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] [[superweapon]] had been stolen and transferred onto the ''Tantive IV'', and Vader aimed to retrieve them. The ''Devastator'' disabled the corvette above the [[planet]] [[Tatooine]] and had soon captured and successfully boarded the [[Starship|ship]]. Once on board, Praji was tasked with quickly making his way to the corvette's main [[computer]] room to search for the [[Death Star]] plans and any other useful information stored in the computer banks before the rebels had the chance to destroy it. To achieve this task he used the modified [[R4 astromech droid]] [[R4-M9]], who was escorted through the ship by stormtroopers with fellow Imperial [[droid]] [[R2-Q2]].''[[Star Wars: R2-D2 Deluxe Book and Model Set]]'' However, the plans had already been removed from the computer by rebel [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]],[[Bloodline (novel)|''Bloodline'']] who then had the [[droid]] [[R2-D2]] carry them off ship in an [[escape pod]]. Once it became clear that the plans were not in the main computer, a report was sent to Vader. Praji later stopped Vader[[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]] and Commander [[Daine Jir]] in one of the ''Tantive IV's'' corridors and reported that the plans were no where to be found on the corvette. He also informed the [[Sith]] that no transmissions had been made by the rebels, but that one escape pod with no life forms aboard had been launched and made it to the surface of Tatooine below. Correctly deducing that the plans must have been in the pod, Vader dispatched Praji to Tatooine, ordering that he personally see to it that a detachment of troops was sent to the planet to recover the plans. The commander then supervised the preparation of''[[Star Wars Complete Vehicles, New Edition]]'' a [[squad]] of [[desert stormtrooper]]s that were then dispatched in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] from one of the ''Devastator's'' hangars. After failing to pick up the plans with their [[scanner]]s, the team landed in [[Mos Eisley]] [[spaceport]].''[[The Odyssey of Star Wars: An Epic Poem]]'' Praji ordered the troopers to begin searching for the plans at once, but ultimately they failed to stop the plans leaving Tatooine in rebel hands. ==Personality and traits== Praji was a light-skinned [[human]] with [[Color|brown]] [[hair]] and blue [[eye]]s. According to [[fleet logistics liaison]] [[Arvira]], Praji disliked filling out datawork.{{StoryCite|story=The Sith of Datawork|book=From a Certain Point of View}} ==Equipment== Praji was equipped with a black [[Imperial military uniforms|military uniform]] including a [[Imperial kepi|kepi]] with an [[Officer's disk|officer's disc]]. He also wore two [[code cylinder]]s and an Imperial [[rank insignia plaque]] with four blue tiles and two amber tiles. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Do you remember your initial impressions of the movie?''"
"''Absolutely. "What is this all about?" I don't think anyone knew ''[what Star Wars was]'', except maybe George Lucas, and I'm not sure he knew all the time!|Scott Chernoff and George Roubicek on Celebration IV|{{InsiderCite|96|The Men Who Built the Empire}}}} Praji was played uncredited by [[George Roubicek]] in the [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], the first installment of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[original trilogy]]. Praji's lines, however, were dubbed by an [[Wikipedia:United States of America|American]] actor, so Praji's voice didn't at all resemble Roubicek's.Ross, Robert (2004). "George Roubicek: Director" (Notes). [[Wikipedia:Monkey (TV series)|''Monkey: The Thirteen Lost Episodes'']] ([[Wikipedia:DVD|DVD]]). [[Wikipedia:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]]: Fabulous Films. In [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], the character's first name was established as "[[Nahdonnis Praji|Nahdonnis]]."{{CCG|set=Premiere Limited|cardname=Commander Praji}} Praji's appearance was in the ''Tantive IV'' sequence shot over three days in [[July]] [[1976]] at the virtual end of Episode IV's principal photography, on the ''Tantive IV'' main corridor set erected on Stage 9 of Elstree Studios, just at the north of [[Wikipedia:London|London]]. Praji was addressed as "Second Officer" in the revised fourth draft script of ''A New Hope'', in which Vader orders fellow officer [[Daine Jir]] rather than Praji to "''send a detachment down''", as shown in the ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Annotated Screenplays]]''.''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''''[[Star Wars: The Annotated Screenplays]]'' During [[Celebration IV]] in [[May]] [[2007]], George Roubicek took part in a round-table discussion chaired by [[Scott Chernoff]], in which he reminisced about his role. His fellow participants were [[Kenneth Colley]] ([[Firmus Piett]]), [[David Prowse]] (Darth Vader), [[Barrie Holland]] ([[Renz/Legends|Renz]]), [[Richard LeParmentier]] ([[Conan Antonio Motti]]), and [[Julian Glover]] ([[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]]). Their conversation was later published in [[Star Wars Insider 96|''Star Wars Insider'' 96]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The Odyssey of Star Wars: An Epic Poem]]'' *{{Film|IV}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: A New Hope (paperback novel)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' junior novelization]] *''[[A New Hope - The Film Novel]]'' {{1stID|Praji}} *[[Star Wars: A New Hope (Disney Die-Cut Classics)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' die-cut children's book]] *''[[Star Wars: The Original Trilogy – A Graphic Novel]]'' *{{StoryCite|story=The Sith of Datawork|book=From a Certain Point of View|audiobook=1}} {{Mo}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga]]'' ==Sources== *{{Topps|set=2017 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Reborn|cardname=#ANH-26 - Commander Praji|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/135181/cid/10118834}} *[[Star Wars: Complete Vehicles (2018)|''Star Wars: Complete Vehicles'' (2018)]] *''[[Star Wars Complete Vehicles, New Edition]]'' *{{EncyclopediaCite|The Rise of the Rebellion and the Battle of Yavin}} *{{LEGOCite|num=75394|name=Imperial Star Destroyer}} ===Non-canon sources=== *[[LEGO Star Wars 119|''LEGO Star Wars'' 119]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |ka=პრაჯი |pl=Praji }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Officers of the Galactic Empire]]
